question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a crafted value into the `hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.record.frequency` Spring Boot configuration property (e.g., via an exposed Spring Actuator `/env` endpoint) to set `frequency` to a duration exceeding 500% of the 24-hour average block time (~2.5s on Hedera), causing `@Scheduled(fixedDelayString = '#{@recordDownloaderProperties.getFrequency().toMillis()}')` in RecordFileDownloader to stall record file polling and freeze visible transaction state on the mirror node?
